title: Quality Gate Orchestration, Failure Semantics, and Required Checks · Changed-Repository Lab
summary: An independent delivery team receives an unfamiliar repository, event, runner, and trust constraint and must transfer quality gate orchestration, failure semantics, and required checks into a new quality gate dependency graph. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of quality gate dependency graph with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define each formatter, lint, type, test, build, security, and policy command with deterministic inputs, exit semantics, output, and owner.
Order cheap deterministic gates before costly work while parallelizing independent evidence and preserving causal diagnosis.
Distinguish success, failure, cancelled, skipped, neutral, timed out, and continued failures in job and required-check policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab Ci Gate Command Exit Contract
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab pipeline hides formatter, lint, type, test, and build failures behind continue-on-error and skipped jobs while branch rules require a renamed check. Rebuild the dependency graph and evidence ownership.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Lab Ci Gate Command Exit Contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define each formatter, lint, type, test, build, security, and policy command with deterministic inputs, exit semantics, output, and owner. For Commit to the mission model · Lab Ci Gate Command Exit Contract, keep Ci Gate Command Exit Contract connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Ci Gate Evidence Summary Ownership
steps.instruction:
Append the CI/CD evidence item headed "  # Evidence: ci-gate-evidence-summary-ownership-1xnncw". Tailor its quality-pipeline.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic workflow or release review, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run GitHub Actions, runner, Docker, registry, OIDC, Google Cloud, and deployment work later in authorized disposable transfer environments.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Ci Gate Evidence Summary Ownership.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Ci Gate Evidence Summary Ownership into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Ci Gate Evidence Summary Ownership, keep Ci Gate Evidence Summary Ownership connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-gate-evidence-summary-ownership-1xnncw
  - competency: ci-gate-evidence-summary-ownership
    artifact: quality-pipeline.yaml
    decision: gate evidence summary ownership
    environment: GitHub-hosted ubuntu-24.04 runner on a cold cache
    fault-injection: change an action tag to a different commit without workflow edits
    retained-proof: queue duration critical path failure class recovery and cost evidence
    case-key: 1xnncw
    change: |
      gates: [format-check, lint, type-check, unit, integration, build, policy]
      order: cheap-before-costly
      parallel: independent-only
      continue-on-error: false
      skipped-policy: explicit-failure
      required-check: stable-quality-name
      summary: command-revision-owner
      rerun: classified-only
    verify: inspect job results required checks skipped paths exact commands and retained failure summaries
    failure: Reject green continued failures, silent skips, mutable required names, log dumps, or unconditional reruns.
    repair: Make exit semantics explicit, preserve dependency order, fail unintended skips, stabilize checks, and assign diagnostic ownership.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Ci Gate Evidence Summary Ownership structural check is only one gate. quality gate dependency graph must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Publish concise annotations and summaries with exact command, revision, environment, failure owner, retained artifact, and rerun limits.

hints: For Defend the transferred model · Lab Ci Gate Evidence Summary Ownership, name the event, revision, actor, permission, runner, dependency, artifact, environment, deployment, and recovery boundary, Ci Gate Evidence Summary Ownership precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Defend the transferred model · Lab Ci Gate Evidence Summary Ownership, trace this concrete input and predict evaluation, shell, job graph, cache, artifact, trust, deployment, signal, rollback, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A lab pipeline hides formatter, lint, type, test, and build failures behind continue-on-error and skipped jobs while branch rules require a renamed check. Rebuild the dependency graph and evidence ownership. Competency probe: Publish concise annotations and summaries with exact command, revision, environment, failure owner, retained artifact, and rerun limits.

For Defend the transferred model · Lab Ci Gate Evidence Summary Ownership, challenge the documented misconception with workflow, run, artifact, policy, fault, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: Dumping complete logs is the clearest and safest failure handoff.
